title: Ctenopteris sikkimensis
intro: Ctenopteris sikkimensis is a type of fern, a vascular plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Ctenopteris sikkimensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ctenopteris sikkimensis is often used to make tea, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
